target.name: Zeta Surgical
target.founder: Jose Amich, Raahil Sha, William Gormley

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $9M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Biotech & Health (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 8 same-sector peers exist in Biotech & Health, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Biotech & Health P/S median is 6.9x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

data.narrative:
Zeta Surgical is a Seed-stage biotech company that has raised $9M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comp universe of 3 public companies in Biotech & Health (RXRX, TEM, ONT.L) and 8 private peers including Colossal Biosciences, Abridge, Chai Discovery, New Limit, and Recursion Pharmaceuticals.

The Stage-Progression method is the primary applicable approach, using total capital raised as a proxy baseline. Applying a 2.5x step-up multiplier—typical for progression from Seed to next-stage rounds in biotech—to the $9M raised yields an implied valuation of $22M. The Peer-Median method is not applicable; while 8 same-sector private peers exist, none meet the filters for narrow peer set construction (same-stage, adjacent-stage, or similar valuation tier), making any all-sector median unreliable. Public-Comp Anchoring cannot be applied because Zeta Surgical's revenue is not on file, which is standard for private-stage companies, and the public sector P/S median of 6.9x cannot be meaningfully deployed without that metric.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $19M to $26M, with a median point of $22M. This reflects a single-method derivation; the primary uncertainty is the absence of comparable Seed-stage biotech transactions and lack of revenue data, which limits triangulation depth. The range incorporates ±15% variance around the stage-progression point estimate to reflect this limitation.
